title: 20s first US screening was Hawaii
wikitext:
[]: 2.0's first U.S. screening was Hawaii (No Spoilers)             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 28, 2009 4:01 am Was browsing the internet and came across this article through Wikipedia: http://www.starbulletin.com/features/celshaded/20091006_Isle_film_fest_gets_Eva_20_first_in_US.html Apparently the (subtitled) film was shown at the Hawaiian International Film Festival last month on October 24th and 25th. I think this hopefully reassuring news that a continental U.S. release is not too far away, also considering it's been screened in Canada.

[]:             Quote Post by Heromaster111 » Sat Nov 28, 2009 4:15 am I saw this the other week and first thing to come to mind was hell that came and went quietly by.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Nov 28, 2009 9:50 am There won't be a "continental U.S. release" until Funi gets around to it in a decade or two. This and Waterloo are film festival screenings and that's all 2.0 has been doing for nowoutside of Asia, very limited, special one-off screenings at specific events. Unless you either have an animation film fest or a regular film fest that has a reputation of bringing anime, there's not real justification for thinking you'll get 2.0 locally any time soon. I know people WANT TO and would really love to see it locally and in a theatre, but hoping for that without considering the current situation will only bring disappointment. I know this may sound dickish coming from a person who has already seen it in a theatre, but it just boggles my mind that people here enjoy setting themselves up for disappointment so often.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 28, 2009 2:16 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: but it just boggles my mind that people here enjoy setting themselves up for disappointment so often.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Nov 28, 2009 4:19 pm You talking about the NYAF? There was some hype that one of the "surprise" anime movies was going to be 1.0, but I was in NYC at the time and it wasn't one of them. I read about the Hawaii screening a month or two back when I was making a front page post and was about to say "this is the first R1 screening of 2.0" but did some fact checking and found out that it was already screened in Hawaii. Since it had already happened, I didn't feel the need to bring it up.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 28, 2009 4:24 pm Ornette wrote: You talking about the NYAF? There was some hype that one of the "surprise" anime movies was going to be 1.0, but I was in NYC at the time and it wasn't one of them.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Nov 28, 2009 6:12 pm ugh I misinterpreted "continental U.S. release" as a "continent-wide release" whooooooops I blame the word "release" for that, since that implies more than just a single screening. Screening within the continental US = plausible before Funi gets it (you can probably try and make that happen too if you have a film fest nearby and befriend/harass the people in charge of programming) Release within the continental US = only when Funi gets it (unless they die and another company takes the license)
